# note: af note --append records a structured note event in the
# workstream's ledger.jsonl (ADR-047). Verified two ways: directly by
# grepping the ledger file, and through af info --ledger.
#
# Failure paths:
#   1. af note without --append is rejected before any state is touched.
#   2. af note --append with no session name, no AF_SESSION, and a cwd
#      outside any worktree cannot infer a session.

env HOME=$WORK/home

# ── Failure 1: --append is required ───────────────────────────────────────────
! exec af note demo
stderr 'note: --append <text> required'

# The rejection happens before session resolution, so no ledger appears.
! exists $HOME/.local/share/af/v1/sessions/demo/ledger.jsonl

# ── Failure 2: no session specified and none inferable ───────────────────────
! exec af note --append 'orphan text'
stderr 'no session specified and none could be inferred'

# ── Success: append a note event to the fixture session ──────────────────────
exec af note demo --append 'hello from testscript'
stdout 'appended note event'

exists $HOME/.local/share/af/v1/sessions/demo/ledger.jsonl
grep '"event":"note"' $HOME/.local/share/af/v1/sessions/demo/ledger.jsonl
grep '"text":"hello from testscript"' $HOME/.local/share/af/v1/sessions/demo/ledger.jsonl

# ── af info --ledger surfaces the appended event ──────────────────────────────
exec af info demo --ledger 5
stdout 'Session:   demo'
stdout 'Recent events:'
stdout '\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2} note'

# ── Initial file content ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- home/.local/share/af/v1/sessions/demo/state.toml --
schema_version = 1

[session]
id = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001"
name = "demo"
status = "active"
created_at = 2026-05-22T10:00:00Z

[worktree]
path = "/tmp/worktree-demo"
branch = "feat/demo"
base_branch = "main"

[execution]
tmux_session = "af-demo"
